description_long: This cowshed features a rectangular structure with a prominent corrugated hip roof in a muted grayish tone, providing ample shelter. The walls are primarily black with a striking geometric pattern in white and gray, giving the building a modern, utilitarian appearance. The front facade displays three large, dark entryways, likely serving as doors for livestock or equipment access. The sides and rear are mostly solid, with the right side showing three horizontal window-like openings, possibly for ventilation or light. The construction appears to use a combination of metal or fiber cement for the roof and concrete or masonry for the walls, with minimal decorative elements. The overall design is functional, prioritizing durability and ease of maintenance, suitable for urban or peri-urban agricultural settings.
description: A rectangular cowshed with a corrugated hip roof, black and white geometric-patterned walls, and large entryways on the front. The structure is modern and utilitarian, with minimal windows and a focus on durability.
description_view_0: Front view showing three large dark entryways and the geometric wall pattern beneath the corrugated roof.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the corrugated roof and a solid black wall, with minimal visible openings.
description_view_2: Rear view with a mostly solid black wall and three horizontal window-like openings on the right side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the corrugated roof and a solid black wall.
attribute_door_style: large open entryways, likely sliding or removable panels, material not fully specified
attribute_window_presence: three horizontal rectangular windows on one side
